title: Editas Showcases Promising EDIT-401 Hyperlipidemia Preclinical Data
text: Editas Medicine reported new preclinical data demonstrating progress of EDIT-401 as potential treatment for hyperlipidemia at the American Society of Gene and Cell Therapy 2026 Annual Meeting. EDIT-401 is an in vivo gene editing therapy using CRISPR/Cas9 technology to upregulate the LDL receptor through permanent modification of hepatocytes. A single dose achieved greater than 90 percent mean LDL cholesterol reduction across all dose groups in non-human primates. The greater than 90 percent mean LDL-C reduction was achieved with only moderate levels of 10-40 percent functional editing of LDLR alleles and greater than 6-fold mean increase in hepatic LDLR protein. Preclinical safety profile showed no adverse clinical observations at therapeutically relevant dose of 1.5 mg/kg. Highest delivery of EDIT-401 observed in hepatocytes compared to other non-target tissues with undetectable oocyte delivery. Company preparing to initiate first-in-human clinical trial of EDIT-401 in patients with heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia later in 2026 with early human proof-of-concept data expected by end of 2026.
